title: RAG is dead, right?? — Kuba Rogut, Turbopuffer
summary: Kuba Rogut argues that RAG is not dead but poorly defined. RAG encompasses vector search, full text search, glob, regex, and iterative agent-based filtering, not just vector lookup. Real usage data contradicts the discourse: Google search volume for RAG spiked in mid-2025 while social media declared it dead.
writeup: Kuba Rogut of Turbopuffer argues that RAG is not dead but poorly defined, covering how the term spans vector search, full text search, glob, regex, and iterative agent-based filtering rather than vector lookup alone. The talk counters the social media claim that RAG is obsolete by pointing to usage data: Google search volume for RAG spiked in mid-2025 even as the discourse declared it finished. The takeaway is that the "RAG is dead" framing rests on a narrow reading of retrieval, and broader retrieval methods remain in active use.
